title: Little Orphan Millie

jokes.text: There is no such thing as an iPoddy.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on modern technology naming conventions
jokes.punchline: Creates absurd product name 'iPoddy' mimicking Apple's naming scheme
jokes.explanation: Solid Homer-style tech confusion with efficient wordplay on Apple products

jokes.text: Modern couch gag 10 couch gags to spice up your marriage
jokes.setup: Traditional Simpsons couch gag setup
jokes.punchline: Meta-joke about using couch gags as marriage advice
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on the show's own opening sequence tradition

jokes.text: There's a bee on Maggie. Okay, Maggie, just calmly and carefully back away.
jokes.setup: Marge giving careful instructions to handle a bee
jokes.punchline: She's talking to a baby who can't understand or follow complex instructions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge mothering instinct applied absurdly to a non-verbal baby

jokes.text: Beehive! Blueberries!
jokes.setup: Baby Maggie responding to bee situation
jokes.punchline: Baby can only say words that start with 'B' sound - beehive/blueberries
jokes.explanation: Clever baby speech pattern joke with alliterative B-words

jokes.text: I told you not to mock me at picnics.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing someone after an incident
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific complaint about being mocked specifically at picnics
jokes.explanation: Homer's specific social boundaries delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Is it that you're brother and sister? Because you really look a lot alike.
jokes.setup: Kirk and Luann announce they have big news
jokes.punchline: Homer's first guess is incest based on their appearance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer inappropriate observation delivered with innocent curiosity

jokes.text: Half-brother and half-sister? ... Siamese twins who've been surgically separated?
jokes.setup: Homer continues guessing after being wrong about siblings
jokes.punchline: Each guess gets more medically absurd and inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation pattern showing Homer's mind going to increasingly bizarre places

jokes.text: Finally you guys will stop using me as a pawn in your fights. ... I made it up. Like kids do.
jokes.setup: Milhouse expresses relief about parents reuniting
jokes.punchline: Admits to lying about being used as a pawn, then deflects with 'like kids do'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Milhouse's psychology and defensive mechanisms

jokes.text: There are so many choices. Bolo? Clip-on? Piano keys? Ten Commandments of Beer?
jokes.setup: Homer struggling to pick a tie for the wedding
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly ridiculous novelty tie options
jokes.explanation: Homer's wardrobe choices escalate to religious beer parody

jokes.text: The cornerstones of the Brewish faith.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his 'Ten Commandments of Beer' tie
jokes.punchline: Creates 'Brewish' as beer-based religion pun on 'Jewish'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly captures Homer's beer obsession in religious terms

jokes.text: You don't know what color my eyes are?! ... Is 'beautiful' a color? No.
jokes.setup: Marge discovers Homer doesn't know her eye color
jokes.punchline: Homer tries to deflect with romantic non-answer that doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer attempt at romantic deflection that backfires immediately

jokes.text: I don't notice the color of people's eyes. I just judge them on the color of their skin.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain why he doesn't notice eye color
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits to racism while trying to defend himself
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Homer making things worse by being accidentally honest about prejudice

jokes.text: Please do not kick sea turtles
jokes.setup: Beach wedding setting
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific environmental warning sign
jokes.explanation: Simple background gag that implies people were kicking sea turtles enough to need a sign

jokes.text: She's wearing white? She must have rolled the odometer back to zero.
jokes.setup: Observing Luann wearing white at her remarriage
jokes.punchline: Car metaphor for resetting relationship mileage/history
jokes.explanation: Clever automotive metaphor for relationship reset that's both judgmental and witty

jokes.text: So you're good at noticing dress colors, but not the eyes that make your dinner.
jokes.setup: Homer commented on Luann's white dress
jokes.punchline: Marge points out the hypocrisy while adding 'eyes that make your dinner'
jokes.explanation: Marge's sarcastic callback with domestic guilt trip about cooking

jokes.text: We are gathered here in the sight of God and this unsafe-for-swimming beach
jokes.setup: Traditional wedding ceremony opening
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions the beach is unsafe for swimming in sacred context
jokes.explanation: Lovejoy's matter-of-fact inclusion of environmental hazard in ceremony

jokes.text: to celebrate the holy un-divorcing of Kirk and Luann.
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony language
jokes.punchline: Creates 'un-divorcing' instead of remarrying
jokes.explanation: Efficient wordplay that acknowledges the specific situation of remarriage

jokes.text: Spaz.
jokes.setup: Someone apparently stumbles or has an accident
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive one-word reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple Bart reaction that's economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: And do you, Luann, take Kirk-- despite the fact that he is fundamentally the same man that you said in court was unfit to load your dishwasher? Whose very touch repulsed you?
jokes.setup: Traditional wedding vow question format
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy reads the brutal divorce court testimony as part of vows
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of wedding vows by incorporating divorce court testimony, creates maximum cringe

jokes.text: Uh, you're reading an early draft of our vows. Just say 'I do.'
jokes.setup: Lovejoy reading harsh divorce testimony
jokes.punchline: Kirk's weak excuse that it's an 'early draft' rather than acknowledging the truth
jokes.explanation: Kirk's pathetic attempt to save face shows his character perfectly

jokes.text: You may now kiss the bride. But make it quick. The seagulls have gotten into the hors d'oeuvres.
jokes.setup: Traditional wedding conclusion
jokes.punchline: Romantic moment interrupted by practical seagull problem
jokes.explanation: Nice juxtaposition of romantic moment with practical beach wedding problems

jokes.text: My friends, do not eat. Is made with seagull meat.
jokes.setup: Wedding reception with food problems
jokes.punchline: Luigi casually reveals the food is made from the seagulls that got into it
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing Luigi's practical solution to the seagull problem

jokes.text: If you don't like Mrs. Simpsons' cooking, you have your 'mommy meals.'
jokes.setup: Milhouse staying with the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Kirk's condescending comment about backup food, showing his poor parenting
jokes.explanation: Shows Kirk's insensitivity and implies Marge's cooking reputation

jokes.text: Why does every kid who stays with us bring 'mommy meals'? So I put pineapple in my potato salad. Live a little, huh?
jokes.setup: Marge questioning why kids bring backup food
jokes.punchline: Defensive about pineapple in potato salad as if it's adventurous cooking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - defensive about her questionable cooking innovation

jokes.text: That's why, to this day, you never see a shark with monkey arms.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently telling a bedtime story
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd evolutionary 'fact' delivered as if logical
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Homer logic presented as natural history fact

jokes.text: Great story, Mr. Simpson. But why do all your bedroom stories have commercials in them for The Container Store?
jokes.setup: Milhouse commenting on Homer's bedtime story
jokes.punchline: Homer apparently includes product placement in bedtime stories
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about Homer's storytelling including inappropriate commercial content

jokes.text: Because if I do it enough, maybe they'll start to pay me. Sleep tight. The Container Store, no wait five convenient locations.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining the commercials in his bedtime stories
jokes.punchline: He hopes to get paid for unsolicited product placement, then does another ad
jokes.explanation: Homer's naive business plan executed even while explaining it

jokes.text: Ah... now that my mom and dad are back together, I'm happy every day. I can't wait to wake up so tomorrow can start.
jokes.setup: Milhouse expressing happiness about parents reuniting
jokes.punchline: Overly optimistic statement that will clearly be proven wrong
jokes.explanation: Sets up dramatic irony - audience knows this happiness won't last

jokes.text: Countdown to wake-up time
jokes.setup: Milhouse so excited about tomorrow
jokes.punchline: He has a literal countdown clock to morning
jokes.explanation: Visual representation of Milhouse's extreme optimism and planning

jokes.text: We're gonna start this marriage off right. This time, I'm gonna carry you over the threshold.
jokes.setup: Kirk wanting to do traditional romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Implies he failed to do basic romantic things the first time
jokes.explanation: Shows Kirk's recognition of his past failures while setting up physical comedy

jokes.text: Dear God! I need more nickels!
jokes.setup: Kirk trying to carry Luann over threshold
jokes.punchline: Apparent vending machine or pay phone emergency interrupts romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Random emergency about nickels interrupting romantic gesture is pure Kirk

jokes.text: Luann, is that you nibbling on my knee?
jokes.setup: Kirk feeling something on his knee in bed
jokes.punchline: Assumes it's romantic when it's clearly something else (animal)
jokes.explanation: Kirk's misinterpretation of animal attack as romantic gesture

jokes.text: Check it out, an animal hole. Where does it go? Only one way to find out.
jokes.setup: Bart discovers animal burrow
jokes.punchline: Plans to investigate dangerous hole in typical reckless Bart fashion
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart curiosity leading to obvious danger

jokes.text: Ah...! Tarantulas! I'll save you, Baby Burps-A-Lot.
jokes.setup: Bart encounters dangerous spiders
jokes.punchline: His priority is saving his doll with ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Bart's heroic priorities focused on absurdly-named toy

jokes.text: Ah...! Spider burps!
jokes.setup: Bart in spider attack situation
jokes.punchline: Worried about spider burps rather than bites
jokes.explanation: Child-like focus on gross bodily functions over actual danger

jokes.text: This week is the best. I don't care if my parent sever come back.
jokes.setup: Milhouse enjoying his time at the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Tempting fate by saying he doesn't care if parents return
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony setup - character says exactly what will cause problems

jokes.text: Milhouse Van Houten? Yes? Son, your parents have been lost at sea.
jokes.setup: Milhouse just said he doesn't care if parents return
jokes.punchline: Immediate karmic punishment - they're actually lost at sea
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony payoff with dark comedy timing

jokes.text: Oh my God! I said I didn't care if they ever came back! This is my fault!
jokes.setup: Milhouse learns parents are lost at sea
jokes.punchline: Immediately blames himself for magical thinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse neurotic response taking responsibility for random events

jokes.text: Drown, monster, drown!
jokes.setup: Milhouse feeling guilty about parents
jokes.punchline: Turns self-hatred outward in dramatic fashion
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's dramatic self-flagellation shows his character perfectly

jokes.text: Hey, who died? My parents, probably. So you guys aren't ice cream men?
jokes.setup: Bart asking about the somber situation
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's matter-of-fact response followed by ice cream truck confusion
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixed with child-like confusion about Coast Guard vs ice cream truck

jokes.text: We'll try, but have you ever been to the sea? It's huge. And we have to search all of them-- 'cause they all, you know, connect.
jokes.setup: Coast Guard explaining search efforts
jokes.punchline: Overwhelming scope of ocean search explained like it's a revelation
jokes.explanation: Professional explaining basic geography as if it's complex, with defeated tone

jokes.text: And we'll move your bedtime to an hour later, so you'll have more time to be alone with your thoughts.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort grieving Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Offers more time alone with thoughts as if that's helpful to a traumatized child
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy showing Homer's complete misunderstanding of child psychology

jokes.text: Hey, hey, come on. Why don't you cheer up with a glass of Ocean Spray?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to cheer up Milhouse about parents lost at sea
jokes.punchline: Suggests ocean-themed drink to child whose parents drowned
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to inappropriate ocean references continues

jokes.text: Cap'n Crunch ... Seven Seas Italian dressing ... Chicken of the Sea tuna? Billy Ocean CD? The History of Atlantic Records?
jokes.setup: Homer realizing Ocean Spray was inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly ocean-related products, each making it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Homer digging himself deeper with each suggestion

jokes.text: Stop naming things! I want to, but I can't!
jokes.setup: Homer listing ocean-related products
jokes.punchline: Homer acknowledges the problem but claims he's compulsive about it
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-awareness combined with inability to stop makes it funnier

jokes.text: Then go to Moe's! Good idea. I'll drown my sorrows in Anchor Steam Beer. Oh! I'm sorry...
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Homer go to bar
jokes.punchline: Homer chooses beer with 'Anchor' name and 'drown' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of ocean theme with 'drown sorrows' and 'Anchor' beer

jokes.text: Like I'm going to a funeral? No! Don't talk like that! There's always hope.
jokes.setup: Marge wanting to dress up Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's funeral reference makes Marge realize the insensitivity
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's dark realism crashes into Marge's optimistic denial

jokes.text: We just wanted to let you know, we've stopped searching.
jokes.setup: Marge just said there's always hope
jokes.punchline: Coast Guard immediately destroys that hope with terrible timing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed hope-crushing delivers maximum dark comedy impact

jokes.text: Just push A, B, up, up, left trigger, right trigger, and both triggers at once.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to get Milhouse to play violent video game
jokes.punchline: Overly complex control sequence for simple violent action
jokes.explanation: Gaming joke about unnecessarily complex controls for simple actions

jokes.text: Isn't there already enough pain in this world?
jokes.setup: Bart encouraging violent video game play
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's existential response to video game violence
jokes.explanation: Profound philosophical question from a child rejecting fake violence due to real trauma

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I've become the world's oldest baby. Men don't get their moo-moo from a ba-ba.
jokes.setup: Milhouse drinking from baby bottle
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary using baby talk ironically
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's self-awareness about regression expressed in baby talk

jokes.text: They get their moo-moo from a big-boy cup!
jokes.setup: Milhouse rejecting baby bottle
jokes.punchline: Still using baby talk while asserting maturity
jokes.explanation: Continues baby talk while trying to be mature, showing internal conflict

jokes.text: I can't be a baby anymore. I'm alone now. I have to be a man!
jokes.setup: Milhouse realizing his situation
jokes.punchline: Child's dramatic declaration of forced maturity due to trauma
jokes.explanation: Touching moment of forced maturity that's both funny and sad

jokes.text: Oh, Marge, darling, what's the combination to our wedding album? Our anniversary!
jokes.setup: Homer asking for combination to locked wedding album
jokes.punchline: Realizes the obvious answer is their anniversary date
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness followed by obvious realization

jokes.text: It's Milhouse! That's not the Milhouse I know.
jokes.setup: Spotting Milhouse at bowling alley
jokes.punchline: Bart notices Milhouse has changed significantly
jokes.explanation: Sets up Milhouse's transformation from nerdy kid to cool loner

jokes.text: Hey, loser, your mom called.
jokes.setup: Bully approaching grief-stricken Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Standard bully taunt becomes horrifically inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy from normal insult becoming accidentally cruel given circumstances

jokes.text: One day, you'll be more haunted by those words than I am.
jokes.setup: Jimbo's cruel joke about Milhouse's mom
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's mature, prophetic response showing wisdom beyond his years
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character development showing Milhouse's transformation into wise, damaged figure

jokes.text: Why aren't you crying? I wish I could cry. Tears would cleanse my soul.
jokes.setup: Jimbo confused by Milhouse's lack of reaction
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's poetic explanation of emotional numbness
jokes.explanation: Profound statement about trauma and emotional numbness from a child character

jokes.text: Go ahead, beat me up. Maybe then I'll feel something.
jokes.setup: Milhouse explaining his emotional state
jokes.punchline: Invites violence as way to feel emotions again
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about depression and emotional numbness seeking physical sensation

jokes.text: Eh, what's the point?
jokes.setup: Milhouse inviting violence
jokes.punchline: Bully finds it no fun to beat up someone who wants it
jokes.explanation: Bully psychology - no satisfaction in beating willing victim

jokes.text: I always wanted you to hold my hand, Lisa. And now that you are, I'm too numb to feel anything.
jokes.setup: Lisa comforting Milhouse by holding his hand
jokes.punchline: Tragic irony - gets what he always wanted but can't enjoy it
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking irony about getting your wish when you can no longer appreciate it

jokes.text: It's like you're wearing oven mitts and I'm in my winter parka.
jokes.setup: Milhouse explaining his numbness
jokes.punchline: Poetic metaphor for emotional disconnection using clothing imagery
jokes.explanation: Beautiful metaphor for emotional numbness that's both touching and funny

jokes.text: That's so poetic.
jokes.setup: Milhouse's oven mitt metaphor
jokes.punchline: Lisa acknowledges the literary quality of his suffering
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the quality of the metaphor just delivered

jokes.text: Hey, I'm happy to see Milhouse get some hand-on-hand action. But no one is cooler than Bartholomew J. Simpson.
jokes.setup: Bart seeing Milhouse getting attention from Lisa
jokes.punchline: Insecurity masked as support, plus formal name usage for gravitas
jokes.explanation: Bart's jealousy and insecurity about losing cool status to traumatized friend

jokes.text: Anyone want to sit over here? You can have my pizza crust.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to regain social status
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to buy friendship with food scraps
jokes.explanation: Sad attempt at social bribery showing Bart's desperation

jokes.text: Please, Bart, you're embarrassing yourself.
jokes.setup: Bart's desperate attempts to be cool
jokes.punchline: Even the bully feels second-hand embarrassment
jokes.explanation: When the bully shows pity, you know you've hit rock bottom

jokes.text: Come on! Look at me, I'm cool. I've got my backpack on frontwards, and I'm krumping!
jokes.setup: Bart desperately trying to prove his coolness
jokes.punchline: Lists uncool things while thinking they're cool, plus dated dance reference
jokes.explanation: Bart's complete misunderstanding of what's cool, plus physical comedy

jokes.text: Check it out! This image has not been speed up.
jokes.setup: Bart doing embarrassing dance moves
jokes.punchline: Caption assures audience the cringe is happening in real time
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke emphasizing how slowly and awkwardly Bart is dancing

jokes.text: There's a time for krumping, and this isn't it.
jokes.setup: Bart's embarrassing dance performance
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's wise, measured response treating krumping seriously
jokes.explanation: Perfect wise Milhouse response treating silly dance form with philosophical gravity

jokes.text: I'll krump with you, sweetie pie.
jokes.setup: Bart failing to get anyone to appreciate his krumping
jokes.punchline: Marge's motherly support in the most embarrassing way possible
jokes.explanation: Marge's well-meaning but socially devastating attempt to support Bart

jokes.text: Milhouse has gone from being a comic figure to a tragic one. If he could go back to being happy, I could go back to being cool.
jokes.setup: Bart analyzing his social situation
jokes.punchline: Selfish motivation disguised as concern for friend
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight showing Bart's selfish but honest psychology

jokes.text: Boy, stop talking to yourself. Your thoughts should stay in your head.
jokes.setup: Bart thinking out loud
jokes.punchline: Homer gives advice about internal monologue while talking to himself
jokes.explanation: Homer's hypocrisy about thought management while narrating his own thoughts

jokes.text: Every Christmas... Milhouse gets Danish butter cookies from... Solvang, California... where he has a beloved un... cle!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to remember family connections
jokes.punchline: Dramatic revelation of obvious solution with suspenseful pausing
jokes.explanation: Bart's detective work presented as dramatic revelation

jokes.text: Butter cookie 8-2147, Hans Christian Anderson 5-5166, and Aquavit 3-2599.
jokes.setup: Operator listing Van Houten phone numbers
jokes.punchline: Phone numbers themed around Danish culture stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Clever theming of phone numbers to Danish cultural elements

jokes.text: Okay, so which one of these losers is Milhouse's Uncle Norbert? Norbert? Norbert? Oh, this guy's a total Norbert.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to identify the right Van Houten
jokes.punchline: Uses 'Norbert' as descriptive adjective for nerdy person type
jokes.explanation: Bart using character name as generic descriptor for nerd type

jokes.text: Norbert? I wish. My name is Gaylord Q. Tinkledink.
jokes.setup: Character responding to Norbert assumption
jokes.punchline: Even more ridiculous name than Norbert with formal middle initial
jokes.explanation: Absurd name that's trying too hard to be funny with toilet humor elements

jokes.text: Well, I'm one of the Danish Van Houtens, not the Dutch Van Houtens.
jokes.setup: Zack explaining his family branch
jokes.punchline: Distinguishes between two closely related European cultures as if vastly different
jokes.explanation: Cultural joke about family pride in specific European heritage

jokes.text: And I'm here to find my nephew Nerdletaub Z. Pantybottom.
jokes.setup: Gaylord Q. Tinkledink explaining his mission
jokes.punchline: Another absurd name combining nerd reference with bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: Weak joke relying on silly name construction without much wit

jokes.text: Let us join forces. Would you be my uncle? No, but have a butter cookie.
jokes.setup: Two characters with uncle-finding missions meeting
jokes.punchline: Absurd offer followed by polite rejection and consolation cookie
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor with Danish cookie stereotype callback

jokes.text: Well, I don't need you. I've learned to take care of myself.
jokes.setup: Uncle Zack offering help to Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Milhouse rejects help claiming independence while clearly still a child
jokes.explanation: Child's assertion of independence that rings hollow given circumstances

jokes.text: You've become quite a young man-- self-reliant, mature. You've shed your Dutch ways and become a true Dane.
jokes.setup: Uncle Zack observing Milhouse's transformation
jokes.punchline: Cultural pride in Danish vs Dutch characteristics
jokes.explanation: Mild cultural humor about Dutch vs Danish traits

jokes.text: You've shed your Dutch ways and become a true Dane.
jokes.setup: Zack praising Milhouse's maturity
jokes.punchline: Cultural transformation framed as personal growth
jokes.explanation: Continues Dutch vs Danish theme as character development metaphor

jokes.text: Oh, man, now he's even more popular. He's troubled, but I can save him.
jokes.setup: Bart seeing Milhouse's continued popularity
jokes.punchline: Recognizes savior complex attraction but frames it selfishly
jokes.explanation: Bart's insight into troubled-person attraction mixed with self-interest

jokes.text: Uh... brown? No. Orange? No. Elm? Rake? Guessing?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to remember Marge's eye color
jokes.punchline: Guesses get increasingly ridiculous, ending with random words and concepts
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperation leads to naming non-colors and abstract concepts

jokes.text: Well, well, look who's strolling down Alzheimer Avenue.
jokes.setup: Barney observing Homer's memory problems
jokes.punchline: Creates street name metaphor for memory loss
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphorical street name for memory problems from unlikely source

jokes.text: A blue bouffant / From here to there / Snow white teeth and lips so red / She's the Wilma to my Fred
jokes.setup: Homer remembering his song about Marge
jokes.punchline: Sweet but simple lyrics ending with Flintstones reference
jokes.explanation: Homer's earnest romantic song with characteristic pop culture reference

jokes.text: Let's see, what rhymes with appraisal? Basil, nasal. None of these are colors.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to remember eye color through song rhyme
jokes.punchline: Focuses on rhyming instead of memory, realizes the flaw in his method
jokes.explanation: Homer's logical process is flawed but self-aware, good wordplay structure

jokes.text: Hazel! Your eyes are hazel.
jokes.setup: Long buildup of Homer trying to remember eye color
jokes.punchline: Finally remembers through hearing Marge's song appreciation
jokes.explanation: Satisfying resolution to long-running eye color mystery

jokes.text: Hazel like the pussy willows by the pond where we first kissed, like the almond paste in the bear claw I ate after we first made love.
jokes.setup: Homer remembering Marge's eye color
jokes.punchline: Romantic memory connected to food memory in typical Homer fashion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment mixing romantic sentiment with food obsession

jokes.text: Oh, did you remember to cut off the gas flow? The what?
jokes.setup: Milhouse completing perfect hot air balloon landing
jokes.punchline: Reveals he forgot basic safety procedure
jokes.explanation: Classic setup where competence is undermined by basic oversight

jokes.text: You're worried you're losing Milhouse, and love is a selfish thing.
jokes.setup: Lisa analyzing Bart's jealousy
jokes.punchline: Wise observation about the selfish nature of love
jokes.explanation: Insightful Lisa wisdom about friendship jealousy and human nature

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Well, when you're mean, I'm a trampoline, so everything you said goes back and hits your ugly head.
jokes.setup: Bart denying he loves Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Reverts to playground comeback that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Bart's emotional regression to nonsensical playground insult

jokes.text: Oh, I love him so much!
jokes.setup: Bart's defensive mechanisms breaking down
jokes.punchline: Finally admits his friendship love openly
jokes.explanation: Emotional payoff as Bart finally admits his true feelings about friendship

jokes.text: Hey, in the real world, rope-climbing skills are vital.
jokes.setup: Someone dismissing gym class rope climbing
jokes.punchline: PE teacher's defensive justification that's obviously false
jokes.explanation: Classic PE teacher delusion about practical value of arbitrary physical skills

jokes.text: Well, what do you think of Mr. Johnson's rope-climbing class now? Still sucks!
jokes.setup: Bart unable to climb rope to reach balloon
jokes.punchline: Proves PE teacher wrong but maintains it still sucks anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback where practical need proves teacher right but Bart maintains attitude

jokes.text: Milhouse! Mom and Dad! Mr. and Mrs. Van Houten! Damned Dutchmen!
jokes.setup: Happy reunion of Van Houten family
jokes.punchline: Zack's cultural prejudice surfaces even in happy moment
jokes.explanation: Danish vs Dutch prejudice callback interrupting touching family reunion

jokes.text: Not so fast, son. We are trapped on this island. No worries. I've already radioed for a rescue boat sailed by brave Danish sailors. More like swishy Danish sailors.
jokes.setup: Rescue situation with cultural pride
jokes.punchline: Cultural rivalry continues with stereotypical insults
jokes.explanation: Continues Danish vs Dutch rivalry theme but feels repetitive

jokes.text: You're going down, Dutchman. You grapple like a girl!
jokes.setup: Cultural tension between Zack and Kirk
jokes.punchline: Physical fight with dated gender-based insult
jokes.explanation: Weak ending relying on tired gender stereotype and repetitive cultural conflict

tmdb_overview: Milhouse stays with the Simpsons when his parents remarry each other and honeymoon on a cruise ship. But when his parents are knocked overboard and lost at sea, Milhouse decides to grow up and be a man.
